title: Extension.js Agent Bridge — Log Event
description: One log line produced by the local dev-time observability plane. Emitted by the in-bundle dev instrumentation, brokered by the dev-server control WS, and written one-per-line to dist/extension-js/<browser>/logs.ndjson. Identical shape on the wire, in the file, and in the in-browser sidebar (lib origin: extensions/extension-js-devtools/src/background/log-central.ts LogEvent). Local only — there is no hosted/emulated backend. THIS IS THE CONTRACT — change it only by bumping `v`.

v.description: Schema version. MUST be the first field and MUST NOT be reordered. The schema-drift guard keys off this.

id.description: Per-event unique id (uuid-v4-ish). Stable across file/wire/sidebar so a reader can dedupe.

seq.description: Monotonic per-session counter assigned by the broker at ingest. THE ordering key — use this, not `timestamp`, to interleave contexts. A jump >1 means events were dropped (cross-check with control-envelope `gap`).

timestamp.description: Producer-side epoch milliseconds. For human display only; clocks can skew across contexts. Order by `seq`.

context.description: Which extension surface emitted the event.

messageParts.description: Sanitized console arguments. Strings truncated and Errors flattened to `name: message` by the producer (8 KB aggregate cap). Never raw objects with cycles.

eventType.description: `dx.signal` is a structured diagnostic (carries code/status/remediation) rather than a plain console line.

code.description: Stable diagnostic code when eventType=dx.signal, e.g. RUNTIME_NO_RECEIVER.

remediation.description: Human/agent-actionable fix carried WITH the diagnostic. The thing CDP console scraping cannot provide.

data.description: Structured payload for dx.signal. JSON-safe (round-tripped by the producer).

url.description: Page/document URL the event originated from, if any.

title.description: Tab title, enriched best-effort by the broker.

stack.description: Stack trace when captured (gated by logger_capture_stacks).

sourceExtensionId.description: The USER extension's id (never the sidecar's own id — self-logs are rejected at the producer).

runId.description: Ties this event to a session in ready-contract.schema.json. Lets a reader scope to the current `extension dev` run.

repeat.description: Present only when the broker coalesced N identical consecutive events under sustained flood. Means this single line stands for `repeat` occurrences.
